REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS JAMIE CHANG AND HIEN NGUYEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 22, 2019. (PARKVIEW VILLAS ASSN., INC. V. STATE FARM FIRE & CASUALTY CO. (2005) 133 CAL.APP.4TH 1197, 1211, 35 CAL.RPTR.3D 411. "[T]HE PROPER RESPONSE, IN MOST INSTANCES, IF THE TRIAL COURT IS NOT PREPARED TO ADDRESS THE MERITS OF THE MOTION IN LIGHT OF THE DEFICIENT SEPARATE STATEMENT, IS TO GIVE THE OPPOSING PARTY AN OPPORTUNITY TO FILE A PROPER SEPARATE STATEMENT....") PLAINTIFFS ARE TO FILE A SEPARATE STATEMENT BY APRIL 5, 2019 AT 5:00 PM. MOVING PARTY MAY FILE REVISED REPLY, BY 5:00 PM ON APRIL 9, 2019. COUNSEL TO GIVE NOTICE. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: S. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS JAMIE SHANG AND HIEN NGUYEN'S DEMURRER TO CAUSES OF ACTION ONE THROUGH SEVEN OF PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. COSTA-HAWKINS AUTHORIZES THE PUBLIC ENTITIES TO REGULATE OR MONITOR THE BASIS FOR EVICTION (SEE CC 1954.52(C)). IN SAN FRANCISCO THE RENT ORDINANCE ENUMERATES SUCH BASES. COMPLAINT ALLEGES (AND ALLEGATIONS MUST BE TAKEN AS TRUE) THAT DEFENDANTS EXERCISED THEIR RIGHTS UNDER COSTA-HAWKINS SPECIFICALLY TO CIRCUMVENT THE EVICTION CONTROL REQUIREMENTS OF THE RENT ORDINANCE. DEFENDANT DOES NOT POINT TO ANY LANGUAGE IN THE ACT, TO ANY AUTHORITY OR TO LEGISLATIVE HISTORY THAT WOULD ALLOW SUCH USE OF THE ACT. THE COURT NOTES THAT THERE ARE NO APPELLATE DECISIONS NO POINT. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS.) JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: PEGGY TSUJIMOTO CSR #5229 (415-828-1919). (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT JAMIE CHANG, HIEN NGUYEN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1-7. DEFENDANT'S UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS #5-6 STATES "[A]T THE TIME DEFENDANTS SERVED PLAINTIFFS WITH THE SECOND RENT INCREASE NOTICE, DEFENDANTS ANTICIPATED IN GOOD FAITH AND UNDER SERIOUS CONSIDERATION THAT IF PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THE NOTICE, DEFENDANTS WOULD SERVE PLAINTIFFS WITH A THREE DAY NOTICE TO PAY RENT OR QUIT. IF PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO CURE THE THREE DAY NOTICE TO PAY RENT OR QUIT, DEFENDANTS WOULD HAVE FILED AN ACTION AGAINST PLAINTIFFS FOR UNLAWFUL DETAINER." (EMPHASIS ADDED.) DEFENDANTS PROVIDED SUFFICIENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN TO PLAINTIFFS, AND PLAINTIFFS DID NOT DISPUTE THESE FACTS FOR THE PURPOSES OF THE MOTION. UNDER BISNO V. DOUGLAS EMMETT REALTY FUND 1988 (2009) 174 CAL.APP.4TH 1534,1553, WHERE "LITIGATION WAS CONTEMPLATED IN GOOD FAITH AND UNDER SERIOUS CONSIDERATION AT THE TIME THE NOTICE WAS SENT" THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE IS TRIGGERED. ACCORDINGLY, DEFENDANTS ARE ENTITLED TO A MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION RESULTING FROM THE RENT INCREASES. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-15-2019 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-22-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Richard Beckman, 415.871.0070 of Beckman, Feller & Chang P.C. for Defendant Jamie Chang.
Jermiah Armstrong, 415.296.7070 of Weisberg & Miller for Plaintiff Igor Gavrilov.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT JAMIE CHANG, HIEN NGUYEN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED as to Causes of Action 1-7. Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts #5-6 states "[a]t the time Defendants served Plaintiffs with the second rent increase notice, Defendants anticipated in good faith and under serious consideration that if Plaintiffs failed to comply with the notice, Defendants would serve Plaintiffs with a Three Day Notice to Pay Rent or Quit. If Plaintiffs failed to cure the Three Day Notice to Pay Rent or Quit, Defendants would have filed an action against Plaintiffs for unlawful detainer." (emphasis added.) Defendants provided sufficient admissible evidence to shift the burden to Plaintiffs, and Plaintiffs did not dispute these facts for the purposes of the motion. Under Bisno v. Douglas Emmett Realty Fund 1988 (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 1534,1553, where "litigation was contemplated in good faith and under serious consideration at the time the notice was sent" the litigation privilege is triggered. Accordingly, Defendants are entitled to a Motion for Summary Adjudication as to all causes of action resulting from the rent increases. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-05-2019 Continued To Apr-22-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jeremiah A. Armstrong, esq. (415-296-7070) of WM Law Firm for Plaintiff party.
No other appearances.

ARGUED: Mr. Armstrong stated parties submitted a stipulated statement, can file a separate statement, and requested a different continuance. The Court instructed counsel to submit filing. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs JAMIE CHANG and HIEN NGUYEN's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is CONTINUED to April 22, 2019. (Parkview Villas Assn., Inc. v. State Farm Fire & Casualty Co. (2005) 133 Cal.App.4th 1197, 1211, 35 Cal.Rptr.3d 411. "[T]he proper response, in most instances, if the trial court is not prepared to address the merits of the motion in light of the deficient separate statement, is to give the opposing party an opportunity to file a proper separate statement....") Plaintiffs are to file a separate statement by April 5, 2019 at 5:00 pm. Moving party may file revised Reply, by 5:00 pm on April 9, 2019.
Counsel to give notice.

Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)

Re Notice Of Defendants Demurrer To Causes Of Action One Through Seven Of Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
William S. Weisberg, esq. (415-296-7070) for Plaintiff/opposing party Igor Gavrilov.
Emily L. Brough, esq. (415-956-8100) of Zacks, Freedman & Patterson for Defendants/Moving party JAMIE SHANG and HIEN NGUYEN.
Court reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto CSR #5229 (415-828-1919).

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's notes). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS JAMIE SHANG and HIEN NGUYEN'S Demurrer To Causes Of Action One Through Seven Of Plaintiffs Complaint is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Costa-Hawkins authorizes the public entities to regulate or monitor the basis for eviction (See CC 1954.52(c)). In San Francisco the Rent Ordinance enumerates such bases. Complaint alleges (and allegations must be taken as true) that Defendants exercised their rights under Costa-Hawkins specifically to circumvent the eviction control requirements of the Rent Ordinance. Defendant does not point to any language in the Act, to any authority or to legislative history that would allow such use of the Act. The Court notes that there are no appellate decisions no point. Order is signed in open court. (See Scanned Order for Details.) Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Court reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto CSR #5229 (415-828-1919). (D501)
